breakfast.name: Blue Moon Bakery
breakfast.address: 120 Big Pine Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
breakfast.note: A Town Center staple - good pastries, solid coffee, and a relaxed pace. Ten-minute walk from the hotel, and a natural first stop before a hike or a day on the mountain.

breakfast.name: Cowboy Coffee Co.
breakfast.address: 25 Town Center Ave, Big Sky, MT 59716
breakfast.hours: Every day, 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM through April
breakfast.note: The locals' coffee shop. Best used as a breakfast handoff in the early part of the day for burritos, pastries, and coffee before the menu shifts into more all-day cafe fare.

breakfast.name: Hungry Moose Market & Deli
breakfast.address: 209 Aspen Leaf Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
breakfast.note: Town Center deli, bakery, espresso, and grab-and-go market. Strong breakfast answer when guests want burritos, pastries, coffee, or something easy to take on the road.

breakfast.name: Unravel Coffee + Bar
breakfast.address: 88 Big Pine Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
breakfast.hours: Thursday - Monday, breakfast 7:00 AM - 11:00 AM (all day coffee)
breakfast.note: Inside Gravity Haus - single-origin espresso and a proper breakfast. The patio opens when the weather's right. Same space turns into a full bar at night.

breakfast.name: Caliber Coffee Roasters
breakfast.address: 80 Snowy Mountain Cir, Big Sky, MT 59716
breakfast.hours: Monday - Saturday, 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM / Sunday, 7:00 AM - 1:00 PM
breakfast.note: Big Sky's only local roaster - voted Best Coffee in Big Sky five years running. Breakfast burritos, baked goods, and specialty espresso drinks. Quick-service and grab-and-go friendly. Hours may shorten in the off-season.

breakfast.name: Cafe 191
breakfast.address: 47995 Gallatin Rd Suite 101, Gallatin Gateway, MT 59730
breakfast.note: A proven local breakfast stop near the entrance to Big Sky. Strong coffee, homestyle plates, and an easy answer when guests are coming from or heading toward Highway 191.

breakfast.name: Hero Snow Coffee
breakfast.hours: April 20 - May 31: Monday - Friday, 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM
breakfast.note: Cozy Meadow Village coffee and pastry stop with handmade drinks, breakfast sandwiches, and a true local feel. Strong answer when guests want a morning stop in the Meadow instead of Town Center.

lunch.name: By Word of Mouth
lunch.address: 77 Aspen Leaf Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
lunch.note: Town Center bistro with weekday lunch and dinner service. Easy walk from the hotel and still one of the better casual local picks in the core.

lunch.name: Chet's Bar & Grill
lunch.address: 44 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
lunch.hours: Daily, Lunch 11:00 AM - 2:30 PM / Dinner 5:00 - 10:00 PM
lunch.note: Basic mountain bar food done well - burger, cold beer, and a seat near the resort. Practical pick when guests are already up at Mountain Village and don't want to drive down.

lunch.name: Terrain Taco
lunch.address: 88 Big Pine Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
lunch.note: Tacos and margaritas inside Gravity Haus with a patio when the weather's right. Locally sourced proteins, creative fillings - a solid lunch especially after a morning on the mountain.

lunch.name: Wrap Shack
lunch.address: 77 Aspen Leaf Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
lunch.note: Fast, casual wraps and bowls in Town Center. One of the easiest lunch handoffs when guests want something quick, inexpensive, and clearly food-first instead of a bar scene.

lunch.name: Blindside Burger
lunch.address: 47 Town Center Ave, Suite D1, Big Sky, MT 59716
lunch.note: Fast-casual smash burger spot from the Blue Buddha and Tres Toros team. Easy lunch handoff when guests want burgers, fries, shakes, and something simple in the core.

lunch.name: Cowboy Coffee Co.
lunch.address: 25 Town Center Ave, Big Sky, MT 59716
lunch.hours: Every day, 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM through April
lunch.note: Useful beyond breakfast. After the morning rush it works as an easy Town Center lunch fallback for paninis, sandwiches, and coffee without sending guests to a bar.

lunch.name: Hungry Moose Market & Deli
lunch.address: 209 Aspen Leaf Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
lunch.note: One of the easiest Town Center lunch handoffs for sandwiches, salads, baked goods, and quick market pickup when guests do not want a full sit-down meal.

lunch.name: The Rocks Tasting Room
lunch.address: 33 Lone Peak Dr #102, Big Sky, MT 59716
lunch.note: Useful when guests want a lighter food stop instead of a full lunch. Flatbreads, soups, salads, charcuterie, and a strong Montana spirits focus make it an easy Town Center handoff.

lunch.name: Scissorbills Saloon
lunch.address: 39 Black Eagle Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
lunch.note: A casual Mountain Village lunch stop with burgers, nachos, and solid ski-day food. Better as a food break than a drinks recommendation.

lunch.name: Beartooth Pub & Rec
lunch.note: Inside Montage - flatbreads from a hearth oven, solid pub food, bowling lanes, darts, and arcade games. Works well for groups or families who want more than just a meal.

lunch.name: Cafe 191
lunch.address: 47995 Gallatin Rd Suite 101, Gallatin Gateway, MT 59730
lunch.note: Good lunch stop for guests driving in or out of town who want a real sit-down meal instead of a quick counter option. Best framed as a Highway 191 pick rather than a Town Center handoff.

lunch.name: The Corral Bar, Steakhouse & Motel
lunch.note: Classic roadside steakhouse and bar south of town. Good lunch answer when guests are headed through the canyon and want a more old-school Montana stop than Town Center.

lunch.name: Hero Snow Coffee
lunch.hours: April 20 - May 31: Monday - Friday, 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM
lunch.note: Good Meadow Village midday stop for coffee, pastries, and lighter sandwiches without sending guests into the busier Town Center core. Best framed as a quick local cafe rather than a full lunch destination.

lunch.name: Mi Pueblito Taco Bus
lunch.address: 1567 Lone Mountain Trail, Big Sky, MT 59716
lunch.note: Fast, authentic taco-bus stop near Ace Hardware. Strong casual handoff when guests want something inexpensive, fast, and local on the way through the Meadow side of town.

dinner.name: Block 3 Kitchen & Bar
dinner.address: 145 Town Center Ave, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.note: On property and easy to recommend - solid food, familiar setting, no need to drive. The natural first suggestion when guests want dinner without the planning.
dinner.rebateNote: Eligible Wilson dining choice. Mention guest dining credit benefits when they apply.

dinner.name: Cabin Bar & Grill
dinner.address: 33 Black Eagle Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.note: A polished Mountain Village dinner with slope views and a quieter room than the busier apres spots nearby. Best when guests want dinner to feel a little more special.

dinner.name: Horn & Cantle
dinner.address: 750 Lone Mountain Ranch Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.hours: Daily, Dinner 5:00 - 9:00 PM / Saloon open from noon
dinner.note: Lone Mountain Ranch dining room - the place when someone asks for the most Big Sky dinner experience possible. Great for birthdays, anniversaries, or guests who want to feel like they're really here.

dinner.name: Cortina
dinner.note: Handmade pasta and a polished room at the Mountain Village. One of the better Italian dinners in the area - right for guests who want something refined without it being a full production.

dinner.name: Montana Jack
dinner.address: 52 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.note: Laid-back Big Sky Resort dinner for families and groups who want something straightforward without the formality. Burgers, bowls, easy patio, and a simple call when guests are already up at Mountain Village.

dinner.name: Wild Caddis at Rainbow Ranch
dinner.hours: April 6 - May 26: Wednesday - Sunday, 4:00 PM - 9:00 PM / Closed Monday - Tuesday
dinner.note: Dinner along the Gallatin River at Rainbow Ranch - log-and-stone dining room, ranch-sourced menu. Worth the drive down the canyon for a special night out.

dinner.name: Riverhouse BBQ & Events
dinner.note: Reliable canyon dinner with smoked meats, river setting, and a strong local following. Best when guests want a relaxed Highway 191 dinner instead of another Town Center stop.

dinner.name: The Corral Bar, Steakhouse & Motel
dinner.note: Classic canyon steakhouse with burgers, steaks, drinks, and a true old-Big-Sky feel. Strong dinner call when guests want a Montana-style meal on the south side of town.

dinner.name: Mi Pueblito Taco Bus
dinner.address: 1567 Lone Mountain Trail, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.note: Casual taco-bus dinner near Ace Hardware with quick service and authentic Mexican staples. Best for guests who want a relaxed, affordable dinner on the Meadow side without a formal sit-down.

dinner.name: Alberto's Mexican Restaurant
dinner.address: 118 Ousel Falls Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.note: Relaxed Town Center Mexican dinner with tacos, enchiladas, and a full bar. Strong walkable dinner answer when guests want something casual, familiar, and easy from the Wilson.

dinner.name: Thai Basil
dinner.address: 112 Ousel Falls Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.hours: Tuesday - Saturday, 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM / Sunday, 11:00 AM - 8:00 PM
dinner.note: An easy Town Center dinner when guests want something outside the usual burger-and-steak rotation. Good for takeout or a casual sit-down without sending them far from the hotel. May shoulder-season schedule may vary by one or two days per week; verify if needed.

dinner.name: Tips Up
dinner.address: 76 Town Center Ave, Unit A1, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.hours: Every day, food service 3:00 PM - 9:00 PM
dinner.note: Big casual Town Center room with fried chicken sandwiches, arcade games, live music, and late-night energy. Strong for groups who want dinner somewhere lively and easy.

dinner.name: Milkie's Pizza & Pub
dinner.address: 77 Aspen Leaf Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.hours: Monday - Friday, 4:00 PM - 9:00 PM / Sunday, 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM
dinner.note: Long-running local pizza-and-pub spot off Aspen Leaf with a more locals-bar feel than the core Town Center pizza stops. Best for casual dinner, takeout, or a laid-back beer-and-pizza night.

dinner.name: Big Sky Noodles
dinner.address: Wilson Hotel Plaza, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.hours: Sun-Mon and Thu-Sat 4:00 PM - 8:45 PM / Closed Tue-Wed / Seasonal fall and winter
dinner.note: Pho and noodle bowls right by the Wilson plaza. Best as a cooler-weather dinner handoff when guests want something warm, quick, and different from the main Town Center staples.

dinner.name: Ousel and Spur Pizza Co.
dinner.address: 50 Ousel Falls Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.note: Family-friendly Town Center pizza spot next to the cinema. A good dinner call when guests want pizza but prefer something a little more sit-down than grab-and-go.

dinner.name: Everett's 8800
dinner.address: 444 Andesite Ridge Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.hours: Lunch daily 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM / Dinner Wed - Sat 5:00 - 9:00 PM (winter)
dinner.note: Guests ride the chairlift up to dinner at 8,800 feet. Alpine tasting menu, seasonal, spectacular views. One of the more unique restaurant experiences in the state - winter only.

dinner.name: Blue Buddha Sushi Lounge
dinner.address: 99 Town Center Ave, Big Sky, MT 59716
dinner.hours: Tue - Sat 5:00 - 9:00 PM / Happy hour 5:00 - 6:00 PM
dinner.note: Big Sky's sushi spot - fresh rolls, Japanese cocktails, pool tables and DJs later in the evening. A good change of pace from the steak-and-burger rotation.

activity.name: Ousel Falls
activity.address: Ousel Falls Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.note: Ten minutes from the hotel and genuinely worth the stop. Easy 1.5-mile round trip through the trees to a 30-foot waterfall. Gets crowded midday - earlier or later in the afternoon is much better.

activity.name: Hummocks Trail
activity.address: Aspen Leaf Dr trailhead, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.hours: Open year-round; best during daylight hours
activity.note: Less than a mile from Town Center and one of the easiest close-to-hotel trail recommendations. Rolling three-mile loop through forest and wetlands, with flexible turnaround points if guests want a shorter outing. In winter it can be icy, so traction is a smart call.

activity.name: Uplands Trail
activity.address: Aspen Leaf Dr trailhead, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.hours: Open year-round; best during daylight hours
activity.note: Shorter than Beehive but still feels like a real outing. Two miles round trip with a steady climb to broad views over Big Sky, Meadow Village, and Town Center. Also less than a mile from Town Center, though guests should know winter conditions can be icy.

activity.name: Lone Peak Trail
activity.address: Access near Ousel Falls Rd & Lone Mountain Trail, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.note: Best framed as a paved walk, run, or bike path rather than a backcountry hike. Guests can pick it up right from Town Center for an easy fresh-air outing, and it now connects toward Community Park through the pedestrian tunnel.

activity.name: Beehive Basin
activity.note: One of the best hikes in the area, full stop. Nine and a half miles round trip with real elevation, ending in an alpine basin surrounded by peaks. Plan for a full day and tell guests to bring layers.

activity.name: Big Sky Resort Scenic Lift Rides
activity.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.note: The gondola runs in summer for guests who want to get up on the mountain without the hike. Great views of the Spanish Peaks from the top - easy to recommend for any fitness level.

activity.name: Adventure Mountain
activity.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.note: The activity hub at Mountain Village - zip lines, mini golf, a climbing wall, and bungee all in one spot. Easy to fill a half day, especially with kids. No need to plan ahead.

activity.name: Waypoint
activity.address: 50 Ousel Falls Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.hours: Movie times and event schedule vary daily
activity.note: The easiest indoor evening recommendation in Town Center. Films and occasional events like trivia or live programming make it a simple post-dinner plan or weather backup.

activity.name: Gallatin River Guides
activity.note: The oldest fly shop in Big Sky and still the best call for fishing. They guide the Gallatin and run trips into Yellowstone. Better framed as a guided outfitter than a drop-in activity.

activity.name: Montana Whitewater
activity.hours: Season opens May 15 / trip times vary daily by booking
activity.note: The Gallatin River runs right through the canyon and Montana Whitewater puts guests on it. The half-day family run is the most popular; the Class IV stretch is legitimate whitewater for guests who want that.

activity.name: Big Sky Resort Ziplines
activity.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.note: Long lines through the trees in Huntley Hollow - the guided nature tour is good for families and the straight zipline run is fast. One of the better ways to see the mountain without hiking.

activity.name: Big Sky Resort Mountain Biking
activity.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.note: Over 40 miles of lift-served trail at the resort, from easy flow runs to technical terrain. Rentals on site. One of the better lift-served bike parks in the West - worth recommending to any guest who rides.

activity.name: Canyon Adventures Horseback Riding
activity.hours: Summer only / guided rides by reservation
activity.note: One- to three-hour guided rides into the Gallatin National Forest. Best framed as a reservation-based summer outfitter rather than a walk-up activity.

activity.name: Lone Mountain Ranch Sleigh Ride Dinner
activity.address: 750 Lone Mountain Ranch Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.note: Sleigh ride out to the North Fork Cabin for a prime rib dinner - about 20 minutes each way through the trees. Books up quickly in peak season. Winter only, and guests remember it.

activity.name: Big Sky Resort Golf Course
activity.address: 2100 Black Otter Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.hours: Summer seasonal / Call for tee times: (406) 995-5780
activity.note: Arnold Palmer-designed, 18 holes at altitude with serious elevation changes and views of the Spanish Peaks. Plays differently than most courses guests are used to - a good challenge.

activity.name: Solace Spa & Recovery Lounge
activity.address: 44 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.note: Full-service spa at the resort - massage, facials, and recovery equipment. Couple's suite available. Good recommendation after a big ski or hiking day. Book ahead in peak weeks.

activity.name: Spa Montage
activity.hours: Spa 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM / Treatments 9:00 AM - 7:00 PM
activity.note: The biggest spa in the area - 12 treatment rooms, heated pools, steam, and an indoor pool. Non-hotel guests can book with a reservation. Right call for guests who take their spa time seriously.

activity.name: Santosha Wellness Center
activity.address: 169 Snowy Mountain Circle, Big Sky, MT 59716
activity.hours: Massage, acupuncture, yoga, sauna, and wellness services - check current schedule or book ahead
activity.note: A long-running local wellness center in Town Center. Best for guests who want something more holistic than a resort spa, with massage, acupuncture, yoga, and recovery-style treatments.

activity.name: Yellowstone National Park Day Tour
activity.note: About 45 miles south and worth a full day. Multiple operators run guided tours covering wildlife, geysers, and hot springs. Having a guide makes a real difference in what guests actually see.

kids.name: Adventure Mountain
kids.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
kids.note: The activity plaza at Mountain Village - zip lines, mini golf, climbing wall, and bungee trampoline all in one spot. No planning needed; easy to fill a half day.

kids.name: Ousel Falls
kids.address: Ousel Falls Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
kids.note: Short, easy trail with a waterfall payoff - kids love it. About ten minutes from the hotel, 1.5 miles round trip. Good for any age that can walk.

kids.name: Big Sky Community Park
kids.note: Open green space with a playground and room to run. Simple and free - the right call when kids need to burn energy without an agenda.

kids.name: Waypoint
kids.address: 50 Ousel Falls Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
kids.hours: Movie times and event schedule vary daily
kids.note: Reliable indoor backup for families in Town Center. Movies and occasional event nights make it an easy evening recommendation when weather shifts or kids still have energy after dinner.

kids.name: Montana Whitewater
kids.hours: Season opens May 15 / trip times vary daily by booking
kids.note: Class II-III runs on the Gallatin - splashy and fun, not scary. All gear provided, and kids genuinely love it. One of the better family half-days in Big Sky.

kids.name: Big Sky Resort Ziplines
kids.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
kids.note: Long lines through the trees at the resort - fast, safe, and great for kids. The guided nature tour version works well for younger ones who want a bit more to it than just the zip.

kids.name: Canyon Adventures Horseback Riding
kids.hours: Summer only / guided rides by reservation
kids.note: Walking-pace trail rides through the National Forest - appropriate for most ages and no experience needed. Better presented as a reservation-based summer outfitter.

kids.name: Yellowstone National Park Day Tour
kids.note: 45 miles south and a full day well spent. Geysers, bison, hot springs - having a guide matters because they know where to be and when. Great for kids of any age.

happyHourDeals.name: The Wilson Lobby Bar
happyHourDeals.detail: Easy on-property happy hour before dinner or after arrival. No need to drive anywhere — right in the building.
happyHourDeals.perk: Natural handoff from the front desk. Guests appreciate the suggestion.

happyHourDeals.name: Block 3 Kitchen & Bar
happyHourDeals.detail: Same building, polished bar. Good call when guests want to settle in for the evening without leaving.
happyHourDeals.perk: Eligible for Wilson dining credit — mention it when it applies.

happyHourDeals.name: By Word of Mouth
happyHourDeals.detail: The Dirtbag Special — a local staple at By Word of Mouth. Easy walk from the hotel, Town Center location.
happyHourDeals.perk: A genuine local pick guests remember. Good for guests who want something less resort-polished.

connect.title: Big Sky Connect - Local Rides
connect.detail: On-demand, fare-free local transit within the Big Sky service area. Request rides at the time of need - no advance scheduling. Does not serve the airport.
bullets: Winter (Nov 24 - Apr 26): Town Center & Meadow Village 6am-11pm / Mountain Village 7:30am-9:30pm

airport.title: Airport Transportation
airport.detail: Big Sky Connect does not serve Bozeman Yellowstone International Airport (BZN). Yellowstone Limousine offers private transfers for the 50-mile trip.
bullets: BZN is approximately 50 miles from Big Sky
Yellowstone Limousine - private car service to/from BZN
Book in advance - demand is high during peak season

hotel.message: For current airport transportation options or help coordinating a preferred local driver, contact the front desk.
